title: The collection should be filtered before sorting by using Where before OrderBy
description: When working with LINQ in C#, it is recommended to pay attention to the order in which methods are chained, especially when using Where and OrderBy methods. It is advised to call the Where method before OrderBy because Where filters the elements of the sequence based on a given condition and returns a new sequence containing only the elements that satisfy that condition. Calling OrderBy before Where, may end up sorting elements that will be later discarded, which can lead to inefficiency.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
var result = data
    .OrderBy(x => x.Name) // Noncompliant - sorting before filtering
    .Where(x => x.IsActive)
    .ToList();
compliant:
var result = data
    .Where(x => x.IsActive) // Compliant - filter first, then sort
    .OrderBy(x => x.Name)
    .ToList();

checks.message: Filter the collection before sorting - use Where before OrderBy for better performance

logic.reasoning: Detects LINQ chains where OrderBy is called before Where. Filtering first (Where) and then sorting (OrderBy) is more efficient because fewer elements need to be sorted.
